name: Sauce Nantua
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a mirepoix of the onion, carrot, shallots, thyme, bay leaf, parsley, salt, and pepper.
2. Sauté the mirepoix in 1/4 cup olive oil until tender.
3. Add crawfish heads and garlic; cook until the heads are red.
4. Add white wine, brandy, fish stock, and tomato puree; simmer for 15 minutes, then strain and discard the heads.
5. Grind the strained heads in a grinder or food processor.
6. Add the ground heads to the liquid and simmer again for 10 minutes.
7. Strain very well and discard the strained heads.
8. Make a roux from butter and flour; add it to the liquid to form a liaison.
9. Finish the sauce with cream, brandy, salt, pepper, and cayenne.
10. Serve over mousseline and garnish with crawfish tails.
